kept.text:
‼️🇮🇱 IMCO Group, an Israeli electromechanical defense manufacturer that produces components for systems including Iron Dome, Rafael, Elbit Systems, IAI, and other Israeli and US military programs, has allegedly been breached, with 30 terabytes of data put up for sale for $500,000.

‣ Threat Actor: Cyber_Isnaad_Front
‣ Category: Defense Industry Breach / Espionage
‣ Victim: IMCO Group (Israel)
‣ Industry: Defense / Military Electromechanical Components
⠀
The actor claims complete penetration of IMCO's network infrastructure, with 10 terabytes of the dataset described as the most sensitive material covering military and defense field components manufactured using the BTP method.
⠀
What's in it:
⠀
▪️ 30 TB total data dump
▪️ 10 TB of high-sensitivity defense and military material
▪️ Production plans and manufacturing data for defense industry components
▪️ Products produced for defense systems including Iron Dome and communication encryption systems
▪️ Work contracts and communications between IMCO and subsidiaries, partners, and major customers
▪️ Customer list reportedly includes Rafael, Elbit Systems, IAI, U.S. Naval Forces, U.S. Air Forces, IDF, and ATENA
▪️ Operational tests, defects, and weak points of specific military-defense products
▪️ Issues raised in internal meetings, including video recordings of management meetings with customers
▪️ PII for 500+ employees across 7 design and production sites in 3 countries
▪️ Photographs from inside factory and production sites
▪️ CCTV camera footage from military-defense and medical fields
⠀
Sale details:
⠀
▪️ Asking price: $500,000

kept.text:
FUSE, part of Elbit Systems C4I & Cyber, will provide its THOR Group 2 UAS to the U.S. Army through a partnership with Mistral Inc., following a contract awarded by the U.S. Army Contracting Command.

The backpack‑portable, rapidly deployable VTOL system is designed to support company‑level tactical missions, providing small units with organic, autonomous aerial capability across a range of operational needs.
Avandra LLC, Elbit Systems’ U.S.-based subsidiary, will support local training, fielding and technical services as part of the program.
Learn more: https://t.co/UunHcNVWVb

profile_bio.description: Elbit Systems is a leading global defense technology company, delivering advanced solutions for a secure and safer world.

kept.text:
@griptmedia On Wednesday, @RBoydBarrett described Daniel Tatlow-Devally as an Irish citizen "languishing in a German prison" for protesting against Elbit Systems, and that Devally engaged in ‘direct action protest’ against genocide.

In reality, Tatlow-Devally has been charged with far more serious offenses...

The illegal break-in of an Israeli arms facility, causing €1m in damage and using Hamas-linked symbols.

He's been charged with trespass, destruction of property, and participation in a criminal organization under section 129 of the German criminal code.

$ONDS Elbit Systems will supply its THOR Group 2 autonomous UAS to the US Army through Mistral, the U.S. defense prime Ondas acquired for $175M just days ago 🔥

The US Army awarded Mistral the contract through Army Contracting Command for backpack-portable, fully autonomous drone systems supporting reconnaissance, surveillance, target acquisition, and cargo delivery at the company level.

Mistral has secured nearly $1.1B in US defense contracts over the past year, including a $982M HERO 120 loitering munition contract, a $73.5M Special Operations Command award, and now the THOR Group 2 program.

Ondas said the acquisition provides "direct prime contractor access to US Army and Special Operations contract vehicles, while adding US-based manufacturing, integration, and federal contracting infrastructure."

Mistral CEO Eyal Banai added: "With the additional scale, technology portfolio, and global reach of Ondas, we are even better positioned to support our domestic defense customers."

Ondas just acquired the company the US Army keeps handing contracts to. 

Probably nothing. 

Thanks for sharing, @DSiegelELNET 🤝
